hypothesis: AMDEN, an EGNN-based diffusion model using VE noise for positions and VP noise for element embeddings (with optional energy-based scoring and HMC refinement), can generate amorphous materials—both single-element silicon across varied thermal histories and multi-element glasses—whose radial distribution functions, bond angle distributions, structure factors, and potential energy distributions closely match those of reference MD-generated samples after local geometry optimization, and conditioning with classifier-free guidance on Young's modulus enables controllable inverse design of mechanical properties.
